name: Oldies Music
description: Sinatra, Motown, Elvis, Aretha — the music your grandparents danced to and your parents still know every word of.

questions.q: Which jazz legend, nicknamed 'Pops' and 'Satchmo', is famous for his trumpet and gravelly vocals?

source.notes: Born August 4, 1901 in New Orleans.

questions.q: Which Frank Sinatra signature song closes with 'And did it my way'?

source.notes: Released 1969; lyrics by Paul Anka, melody from the French song 'Comme d'habitude'.

questions.q: Before they became The Supremes, what was Diana Ross's group originally called?

source.notes: Renamed in 1961 when they signed to Motown.

questions.q: Who recorded the 1970 protest song 'War (What is it Good For?)'?

source.notes: Reached #1 on the Billboard Hot 100.

questions.q: What is the common name for the group of musicians (including Hendrix, Joplin, and Cobain) who all died at the same age?

source.notes: Includes Brian Jones, Hendrix, Joplin, Morrison, Cobain, and Amy Winehouse.

questions.q: Who was Elvis Presley's longtime manager, known by his self-styled military rank?

source.notes: Born Andreas Cornelis van Kuijk in the Netherlands; the 'Colonel' was honorary.

questions.q: What was the name of Smokey Robinson's Motown vocal group?

source.notes: First Motown group to score a million-selling record.

questions.q: Which 1958 instrumental hit by The Champs is named after a Mexican spirit?

source.notes: Reached #1; the only word in the song is its title.

questions.q: Whose 1968 recording of 'I Heard It Through the Grapevine' became a Motown signature?

source.notes: Gladys Knight & the Pips also had a hit version a year earlier.

questions.q: Who recorded the 1969 hit 'Spirit in the Sky'?

source.notes: Greenbaum is one-hit-wonder famous; the song still appears in films constantly.

questions.q: Which Quentin Tarantino film famously features Stealers Wheel's 'Stuck in the Middle with You'?

source.notes: Used in the ear-cutting scene by Mr. Blonde (Michael Madsen).

questions.q: What instrument is Nat King Cole most associated with, in addition to his velvet vocals?

source.notes: Led the King Cole Trio; was a respected jazz pianist before turning to vocals.

questions.q: Whose 1967 anthem demanded 'Respect' (R-E-S-P-E-C-T)?

source.notes: Originally an Otis Redding song; Aretha's 1967 cover became the definitive version.

questions.q: Which group's 1962 instrumental 'Green Onions' became a soul/blues classic?

source.notes: House band for Stax Records; Booker T. Jones on Hammond organ.

questions.q: Which Monkees member, the 'cute one' from Manchester, England, died in 2012?

source.notes: Died February 29, 2012, age 66.

questions.q: Which female vocal group's 1954 hit 'Mr. Sandman' is famous for its 'Bung-bung-bung-bung' intro?

source.notes: Reached #1 on the Billboard chart in 1954–55.

questions.q: Elvis Presley's first single, 'That's All Right', was released in what year?

source.notes: Recorded at Sun Studio in Memphis on July 5, 1954.

questions.q: In which US state was Elvis Presley born?

source.notes: Born in Tupelo, MS, on January 8, 1935. Family moved to Memphis when he was 13.

questions.q: Who wrote the song 'White Christmas', made famous by Bing Crosby?

source.notes: Bing Crosby's recording is one of the best-selling singles in history.

questions.q: Which folk singer was Bob Dylan's primary musical hero, prompting his move to New York City?

source.notes: Dylan visited Guthrie in his hospital bed in 1961 and wrote 'Song to Woody'.
